ddls:
CREATE TABLE "companies" (
  "company_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each company */,
  "company_name" TEXT /* Name of the company */,
  "industry" TEXT /* Industry sector the company operates in */,
  "headquarters" TEXT /* Headquarters location of the company */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for inquiries about job listings */,
  "website" TEXT /* Company website */,
  "company_size" TEXT /* Size of the company (small, medium, large) */,
  "company_type" TEXT /* Type of company (public, private, startup) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("company_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "locations" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each location */,
  "city_name" TEXT /* Name of the city */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country where the city is located */,
  "region" TEXT /* Region within the country (e.g., state, province) */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the location */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the location */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "job_levels" (
  "level_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each job level */,
  "level_name" TEXT /* Name of the job level (e.g., L5, Senior) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the responsibilities at this job level */,
  "average_salary" INTEGER /* Average salary for this job level */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("level_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "job_tags" (
  "tag_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each tag */,
  "tag_name" TEXT /* Name of the tag */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the tag's relevance */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tag_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "salary_data" (
  "salary_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for salary data entry */,
  "job_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the job listing to which this salary data pertains */,
  "base_salary" INTEGER /* Base salary offered for the job */,
  "stock_grant" INTEGER /* Stock grant offered with the job */,
  "bonus" INTEGER /* Bonus offered with the job */,
  "average_salary" INTEGER /* Average salary for similar jobs */,
  "max_salary" INTEGER /* Maximum salary for similar jobs */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("salary_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_profiles" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the user */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user's location */,
  "years_of_experience" INTEGER /* Total years of experience the user has */,
  "resume" TEXT /* User's resume */,
  "cover_letter" TEXT /* User's cover letter */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_profiles_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES locations ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "job_applications" (
  "application_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each job application */,
  "job_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the job listing being applied for */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user submitting the application */,
  "application_date" TEXT /* Date the application was submitted */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the application (e.g., pending, rejected, accepted) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("application_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_applications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES user_profiles ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "skills" (
  "skill_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each skill */,
  "skill_name" TEXT /* Name of the skill */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the skill's relevance */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("skill_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "job_skills" (
  "job_skill_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each job skill entry */,
  "job_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the job listing requiring the skill */,
  "skill_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the required skill */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("job_skill_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_skills_skill_id FOREIGN KEY ("skill_id") REFERENCES skills ("skill_id")
)
